(2) In this section the expressions "public order, public faith or good government" shall, without prejudice to their generality, include the following:

(a) the responsibility of the Union as a component part of the British Empire, and all questions relating to defence, including provision, construction, maintenance and use of defence works, areas and communications;

(b) foreign relations and relations with other parts of the British Empire;

(c) subjects upon which, in the opinion of the Governor or of a Secretary of State, it is necessary to secure uniformity in legislation between the Union and the Colony of Singapore; and

(d) all matters pertaining to the creation or abolition of any public office and to the appointment, salary or other conditions of service or any public officer or officers.

(3) The Governor shall forthwith report to a Secretary of State every case in which he shall make any such declaration as is mentioned in sub-section (1) of this section and the reason therefor.

(4) If any Member of the Legislative Council objects to any declaration made under this section, he may, within seven days of the making thereof, submit to the Governor a statement in writing of his reasons for so objecting and a copy of such statement shall, if furnished by such Member, be forwarded by the Governor as soon as practicable to a Secretary of State.

(5) Any such declaration other than a declaration relating to a resolution, may by a Secretary of State, and the Governor shall notify such revocation in the Gazette; and from the date of such notification any resolution which shall have had effect by virtue of the declaration revoked shall cease to have effect, but without prejudice to anything lawfully done thereunder.

31. (1) No Bill shall become a law until either the Governor shall assent thereto in His Majesty's name and on His Majesty's behalf, and shall sign the same in token of assent, or His

/Majesty

===== Page 501 =====
Majesty shall give his assent thereto through a Secretary of State.

(2) When a Bill is presented to the Governor for his assent, he shall, according to his discretion, but subject to the provisions of this Order and of any Instructions addressed to him under His Majesty's Sign Manual and Signet or through a Secretary of State, declare that he assents or refuses to assent thereto, or that he reserves the Bill for the signification of His Majesty's pleasure.

(3) A law assented to by the Governor shall be published in the Gazette and shall come into operation on the date on which such assent shall be given, or, if it shall be enacted either in the law or in some other enactment (including any enactment in force at the commencement of this Order) that it shall come into operation on some other date, on that date.

(4) A Bill reserved for the signification of His Majesty's pleasure shall become a law so soon as His Majesty shall have given His assent thereto, through a Secretary of State, and the Governor shall have signified such assent by Proclamation in the Gazette. Every such law shall come into operation on the date of such Proclamation or, if it shall be enacted either in the law or in some other enactment (including any enactment in force at the commencement of this Order), that it shall come into operation on some other date, on that date.

32. (1) Any law to which the Governor shall have given his assent may be disallowed by His Majesty through a Secretary of State.

(2) Whenever any law has been disallowed by His Majesty, the Governor shall cause notice of such disallowance to be published in the Gazette.

(3) Without prejudice to anything lawfully done thereunder, every law so disallowed shall cease to have effect as soon

===== Page 502 =====
The Council, and the same shall be debated and disposed of according to the Standing Rules and Orders.

Provided that, except with the recommendation or consent of the Governor signified thereto, the Council shall not proceed upon any Bill, amendment, motion, or petition, which

===== Page 503 =====
As notice of such disallowance shall be published as aforesaid. Thereafter, any enactment repealed or amended by such law shall have effect as if such law had not been made, but save as aforesaid the provisions of subsection (2) of section 38 of the Interpretation Act, 1889 shall apply to such disallowance as they apply to the repeal of an Act of Parliament.

33. Subject to the provisions of this Order, the Governor and the Council shall, in the transaction of business and the passing of laws, conform as nearly as may be to the directions contained in any Instructions under His Majesty's Sign Manual and Signet which may from time to time be addressed to the Governor in that behalf.

34. (1) Subject to the provisions of this Order and of such Instructions as aforesaid, the Council may, from time to time, make, amend and revoke Standing Rules and Orders for the regulation and orderly conduct of its own proceedings and the conduct of business and for the passing, intitling and numbering of Bills, and for the presentation of the same to the Governor for his assent, but no such Rules or Orders shall have effect unless and until they have been approved by the Governor.

35. Subject to the provisions of this Order and of the Standing Rules and Orders of the Council, any Member may introduce any Bill or propose any motion for debate in, or may present any petition, which, in the opinion of the Governor or of the presiding Member, would—

(a) dispose of, or charge any public revenue or public funds of the Union, or revoke or alter any disposition thereof or charge thereon, or impose, alter, or repeal any rate, tax or duty; or

(b) suspend the Standing Rules and Orders of the Council or any of them.

===== Page 504 =====
36. (1) The sessions of the Legislative Council shall be held at such times and places as the Governor shall from time to time by proclamation appoint.

(2) There shall be a session of the Council at least once in every year, so that a period of twelve months shall not intervene between the last sitting in one session and the first sitting in the next session.

37. (1) The Governor may at any time, by proclamation, prorogue or dissolve the Legislative Council.

(2) On dissolution, all Members shall vacate their seats and the Council shall be reconstituted by election and appointment in accordance with the provisions of this Order at such time, within three months of every dissolution, as the Governor shall by proclamation appoint.

===== Page 505 =====
PART V. THE COUNCIL OF SULTANS.

38. There shall be established for the Union a Council to be called the Council of Sultans.

39. (1) Subject to the provisions of this Order, the Council of Sultans shall consist of The Governor as President and the following Members:

His Highness the Sultan of the State and Territory of Johore;

His Highness the Sultan and Ruler of Kedah;

His Highness the Raja of Kelantan;

His Highness the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan;

His Highness the Sultan of Pahang;

His Highness the Sultan of Perak;

His Highness the Raja of Perlis;

His Highness the Sultan of Selangor;

His Highness the Sultan of Trengganu;

The Attorney General; and

The Chief Secretary; and

The Financial Secretary.

(2) During a Regency in any of the Malay States, the Regent or, if there be more than one, such one of the Regents as His Majesty may assign by instructions under His Sign Manual and Signet or through a Secretary of State, shall be a Member of the Council in the place of the Sultan of that State.

40. The functions of the Council of Sultans shall be to advise the Governor on all matters affecting the Mahommedan religion and, in particular, upon Bills enacted by a Sultan with the advice of the Malay Advisory Council in any State, requiring the assent of the Governor under Part VII hereof;

(b) to advise the Governor on any matter referred to the Council by the Governor for discussion; and

===== Page 506 =====
(c) With the prior consent of the Governor, to advise the Governor on any matter proposed by any Sultan for discussion.

41. Whenever, upon any special occasion, the Governor desires that the Council of Sultans shall have the advice of any person in the Union upon any matter before the Council, he may summon for such special occasion any such person as an Extraordinary Member of the Council.

Extraordinary Members shall not have the right to vote on any matter before the Council.

42. The Members of the Council of Sultans shall take precedence among themselves as follows:

First, the Sultans in order according to the dates of their recognition by His Majesty as Sultans of their respective States;

Second, Regents in order according to the dates of their appointments as Regents;

Third, The Chief Secretary, the Attorney General and the Financial Secretary in that order; and

Fourth, the Extraordinary Members in such order as the Governor shall assign.

43. The seat of a Sultan shall become vacant:

(a) Upon the death of the Sultan;

(b) Upon the death of any Regent occupying a seat;

(c) If he shall cease to be Sultan of his State, or if a Regent occupying the seat shall cease to be the Regent assigned a Member of the Council under the provisions of Section (2) of this Order;

(d) If he or Regent occupying the seat shall take any oath, or make any declaration or acknowledgment, of allegiance or adherence to any foreign Power or State; or shall do, concur in, or adopt any act done with the intention that he shall become a subject or citizen of, or enter or attempt to enter into diplomatic relations with, any foreign Power or State; or

(e) If, under any law in force in the Union, he or the Regent occupying the seat shall be found or declared to be of unsound mind or adjudged to be a lunatic;

(f) If he, or the Regent occupying the seat shall be declared by the Governor, under the provisions of subsection (2) of this section, to be incapable of discharging his functions as a Member of the Council.

(2) The Governor may, by an Instrument under the Public Seal, declare any Member of the Council of

===== Page 507 =====
Sultans to be incapable of discharging his functions as a Member of the Council, and, thereupon, such Member shall not sit or vote in the Council. Every such declaration shall forthwith be reported to His Majesty through a Secretary of State and shall remain in force until such Member shall be declared in manner aforesaid to be again capable of discharging his said functions.

(3) Any person vacating a seat as a Member of the Council may, if qualified, be again appointed from time to time.

44. (1) Whenever the seat of a Member of the Council of Sultans is temporarily vacant, the Governor may, by Instrument under the Public Seal, appoint a person to be a Temporary Member for the period of such vacancy.

(2) The Governor shall forthwith report every such temporary appointment to His Majesty through a Secretary of State. Any such appointment may (without prejudice to anything done by virtue thereof) be disallowed by His Majesty through a Secretary of State or revoked by the Governor by Instrument under the Public Seal.

(3) A temporary appointment under this section shall cease to have effect on notification by the Governor to the person appointed of disallowance by His Majesty or of revocation by the Governor or on supervision of the appointment by the definitive appointment of a person to fill the vacancy, or where the vacancy shall otherwise cease to exist.

45. The Governor shall preside at meetings of the Council of Sultans.

46. All questions proposed for decision in the Council may be determined by a majority of the votes of the Members present and Voting. The President shall not have

===== Page 508 =====
47. No business except that of adjournment shall be transacted if objection is taken by any Member that there are less than five Members present.

48. The official languages of the Council of Sultans shall be Malay and English.

49. Subject to the provisions of this Order, the Council may, from time to time, make, amend and revoke Standing Rules and Orders for the regulation and orderly conduct of its proceedings and the despatch of business but no such Standing Rules and Orders shall have effect until they have been approved by the Governor.

50. Subject to the provisions of this Order the Council of Sultans shall, in the transaction of business, conform as nearly as may be, to the directions contained in any Instructions under His Majesty's Sign Manual and Signet which may from time to time be addressed to the Governor in that behalf.

51. (1) The Sessions of the Council of Sultans shall be held at such time and places as the Governor shall, from time to time, by proclamation appoint.

(2) There shall be a session of the Council at least once in every year, so that a period of twelve months shall not intervene between the last sitting in one Session and the first sitting in the next Session.

===== Page 509 =====
PART VI STATE AND SETTLEMENT COUNCILS

Establishment of State and Settlement Councils

52. (1) There shall be established for each of the Malay States a State Council.

(2) There shall be established in and for each of the Settlements a Settlement Council.

53. Each State Council and Settlement Council shall consist of the Resident Commissioner as Chairman and such ex-officio Members, Nominated Official Members, Nominated Unofficial Members and Elected Members as may be specified by any law in force in the Colony or prescribed by regulations made by the Governor under Section 104 of this Order.

54. (1) Subject to the provisions of this Order, it shall be lawful for a State Council or Settlement Council to make laws on any subject -
(a) which, in the opinion of the Governor in Council is of a purely local or private nature in the State or settlement;
or
(b) in respect to which the Legislative Council shall have delegated powers of legislation to the State or Settlement Council concerned.

(2) A State Council or Settlement Council may exercise such powers of local administration as may from time to time be allocated to it by the Government of the Union.

Nominated Members

55. The Nominated Members shall be appointed by the Governor by Instrument under the Public Seal.

Elected Members

56. The Elected Members shall be elected in the manner provided by any law for the time being in force in the Union or by Regulations made by the Governor under Section 104 of this Order.

===== Page 510 =====
57. Whenever upon any occasion the Governor is of opinion that a State Council or Settlement Council should have the assistance of any person touching any matter, he may, by Instrument under the Public Seal, appoint such person to be, for such occasion, an Extraordinary Member of the Council, but no such Extraordinary Member shall have the right to vote in the Council.

58. (1) Subject to the provisions of the Order, a person appointed a Nominated Member shall vacate his seat at the expiration of three years from the date of his appointment, or on the dissolution of the Council whichever is earlier, but, if qualified, shall be eligible for reappointment.

(2) If any person is appointed temporarily a Member of a State Council or Settlement Council in accordance with the provisions of Section 6 of this Order and such appointment is immediately followed by his definitive appointment as a Nominated Member, the said period of three years shall be reckoned from the date of his appointment as a temporary member.

59. Subject to the provisions of this Order, a person elected as an Elected Member shall vacate his seat at the expiration of three years from the date of his election, or on dissolution of the Council, whichever is earlier, but, if qualified, shall be eligible for re-election.

60. Subject to the provisions of this Order, any person who is a British nominated subject or a Malayan Union citizen, who is of the age of twenty-one years or upwards shall be qualified to be an elected or nominated member of a State Council or Settlement Council, and no other person shall be qualified to be appointed or elected thereto, or, having been so elected

===== Page 511 =====
61. (1) A person shall be disqualified for nominated membership of any State Council or Settlement Council for any reason which would cause disqualification for Unofficial Members of the Legislative Council under the provisions of section 20 of this Order.

(2) A person shall be disqualified for Elected Membership of a State Council or Settlement Council for any reason which would cause disqualification of a Member of the Legislative Council under paragraph (1), (2), or (4) of section 21 of this Order, or

(a) if, being a party to, or a member of a firm or director or manager of a company which is a party to, or interested in any subsisting contract with the Government of the Union or of any State or Settlement thereof for or on account of the public service, he shall not have published within one month before the day of election, in the Gazette or in some newspaper circulating in the State or Settlement in which he seeks election, a notice setting out the nature of such contract and his interest therein; or

(b) if he is disqualified for membership of the Council under any law or regulation for the time being in force in the State or Settlement concerned relating to offences connected with the election of Members.

62. (1) The seat of a Nominated Official Member or Nominated Unofficial Member of a State Council or Settlement Council shall become vacant upon the occurrence in relation to such Member of any event which, if it occurred in relation to a Nominated Official Member or Nominated Unofficial Member of the Legislative Council, would cause the seat of such last mentioned Member to become vacant under the provisions of sub-section (2) of section 22 of this Order.

===== Page 512 =====
(2) The seat of an Elected Member of a State or Settlement Council shall become vacant upon the occurrence in relation to such Member of any event which would cause the seat of a Nominated Member to become vacant under the provisions of sub-section (1) of this section, or if such Elected Member is disqualified for membership of the Council under any law or regulation for the time being in force in the State or Settlement concerned relating to offences connected with the election of Members.

(3) If any Nominated Unofficial Member or Elected Member shall be appointed temporarily to, or to act in, any office of emolument under the Crown in the Union, he shall not sit or vote in the Council so long as he continues to hold, or act in that office.

(4) The Governor may, by Instrument under the Public Seal, declare any Nominated Member to be incapable of discharging his functions as a Member of the State Council or Settlement Council concerned and thereupon such Member shall not sit or vote in the Council until he is declared, in manner aforesaid, again to be capable of discharging his said functions.

(5) Whenever the seat of a Nominated Unofficial Member or Elected Member becomes vacant under sub-section (1) or sub-section (2) of this section, the vacancy shall forthwith be reported to the Governor in writing by the Clerk of the Council concerned.

63. (1) Subject to the provisions of this Order, all questions which may arise as to the right of any person to be or remain a Nominated Member of a State Council or Settlement Council shall be referred to and decided by the Governor in Council.

(2) Subject as aforesaid, all questions which may arise as to the right of any person to be or remain an Elected Member shall be determined as may be prescribed.

===== Page 513 =====
By any law of the Union relating to the election of Members or by regulations made by the Governor under section 104 of this Order.

64. (1) Whenever there shall be a vacancy in the number of persons sitting in any State or Settlement Council as ex officio Members, or Nominated Official Members, or Nominated Unofficial Members, or Elected Members, by reason of the fact that:

(a) one person is lawfully discharging the functions of more than one of the ex officio Members; or

(b) a Nominated Official Member is lawfully discharging the functions of the office of an ex officio Member; or

(c) no person is lawfully discharging the functions of such an official; or

(d) a Nominated Official Member or Nominated Unofficial Member is suspended from the exercise of his functions as a Member; or

(e) the seat of a Nominated Official Member or Nominated Unofficial Member is vacant for any cause other than a dissolution of the Council; or

(f) a Member is unable to sit or vote in the Council in consequence of a declaration by the Governor, as provided in this Order, that he is incapable of discharging his functions as a Member; or

(g) a Member is absent from the Union; or

(i) a Nominated Unofficial Member is unable to sit or vote in the Council in consequence of his having been appointed temporarily to an office of emolument under the Crown in the Union or to act in any such office;

===== Page 514 =====
The Governor may, by Instrument under the Public Seal, appoint a person to be temporarily a Member for the period of such vacancy.

(2) If the vacancy is in the number of persons sitting in the Council as ex officio Members or Nominated Official Members, the person appointed shall be a person qualified for appointment as a Nominated Official Member; and if the vacancy is in the number of persons sitting in the Council as Nominated Unofficial Members, the person appointed shall be a person qualified for appointment as a Nominated Unofficial Member.

(3) A temporary appointment under this section shall cease to have effect on notification by the Governor to the person appointed of revocation by the Governor of the appointment or on supersession of the appointment by the definitive appointment of a person to fill the vacancy or when the vacancy shall cease to exist.

65. The Chairman, if present, or in the absence of the Chairman, such Member of the Council as the Resident Commissioner may from time to time appoint or, in default of such appointment, or in the absence of the Member so appointed, the Member present who stands first in order of precedence, shall preside at the meeting of the State Legislative Council and Settlement Councils.

66. (1) After the Chairman, the Members of each State Council and Settlement Council shall take precedence among themselves as the Governor may specially assign, and the default thereof as follows:

First the ex officio Members in the order in which they are mentioned in any law or regulation made pursuant to section 104 hereof;

Secondly, the Nominated Members and Elected Members according to the length of time during which they have been continuously Members of the Council; Members elected or appointed at the same time taking precedence.

===== Page 515 =====
Amongst themselves as the Resident Commissioner may assign.

(2) For the purposes of this section -
(a) When the State Council or Settlement Council is dissolved, Members elected at the ensuing elections shall be deemed to have been elected by virtue of those elections, and Nominated Members appointed to fill vacancies caused thereby shall be deemed to have been appointed, on the date on which the report of the first successful candidate for election is made to the Governor;

(b) Paragraph (a) of this sub-section shall apply to the Members elected or appointed to a State Council or Settlement Council as first constituted under this Order as if such elections or appointments were consequent upon a dissolution of the Council; and

(c) In ascertaining the period during which a person has continuously been an Elected Member or a Nominated Member of the Council no account shall be taken of any interval between the vacation by that person of his seat in the Council in consequence of the expiration of his tenure of office or a dissolution of the Council and the date of his re-election or re-appointment to fill a vacancy in the Council caused by that expiration of tenure or that dissolution.

67. (1) Whenever the seat of a Nominated Member becomes vacant, the vacancy shall be filled by appointment by the Governor.

(2) Whenever the seat of an Elected Member becomes vacant, a fresh election shall be held to fill the vacancy in accordance with section 104 of this Order.

===== Page 516 =====
Voting.
68. Save as otherwise provided in this Order, all questions proposed for decision in the Council shall be determined by a majority of the votes of the Members present and voting. The Chairman shall not have an original vote, but if upon any question the votes shall be equally divided, he shall have a casting vote.

Assent to Bills. Disallowance of laws.
69. The provisions of sections 31 and 32 of this Order (which relate respectively to assent to Bills and disallowance of laws passed by the Legislative Council) shall apply to Bills and to laws passed by a State Council or Settlement Council.

Conflict.
70. In the event of any conflict between a law passed by any State Council or Settlement Council and any Ordinance of the Legislative Council, the provision of the Ordinance shall prevail.

Royal Instructions.
71. Subject to the provisions of this Order a State Council or Settlement Council shall, in the transaction of business and the passing of laws, conform as nearly as may be to the directions contained in any Instructions under His Majesty's Sign Manual and Signet which may from time to time be addressed to the Governor and to any instructions of the Governor under the Public Seal which may from time to time be addressed to the Resident Commissioner, in that behalf.

Standing Orders.
72. The provisions of sections 34, 36, 37 and 38 of this Order (which relate respectively to Standing Orders of the Legislative Council, the introduction of Bills in, sessions of, and Prorogation and dissolution of that Council) shall apply also to State Councils and Settlement Councils, and shall have effect as if the words "any State Council or Settlement Council" were substituted for the words "the Legislative Council" wherever those words appear.

Official Language.
73. (1) The official languages of each State Council shall be Malay and English.

(2) The official language of each Settlement Council shall be English.

===== Page 517 =====
PART VII. MALAY ADVISORY COUNCILS.

Establishment of Malay Advisory Councils

74. There shall be established for each of the Malay States a Council to be called the Malay Advisory Council.

Membership.

75. Each Malay Advisory Council shall consist of the Sultan as President and such Members as the Sultan, with the approval of the Governor, shall appoint.

Functions.

76. The functions of each Malay Advisory Council shall be:
(a) to advise the Sultan on all matters affecting the Mahommedan religion;
(b) to advise the Sultan on all matters which are referred to the Council by the Resident Commissioner with the approval of the Governor;
(c) subject as hereinafter mentioned to advise the Sultan in the enactment of laws, to have effect within the State concerned, on matters of Mahommedan religion.

Power to make laws on matters of Mahommedan religion.

77. (1) Subject to the provisions of this Order, it shall be lawful for the Sultan of each of the Malay States, with the advice of the Malay Advisory Council of such State, to enact laws relating solely to matters of Mahommedan religion excluding any matter involving the imposition, collection or remission of any tax or tithe.

(2) Such laws shall have effect only within the State concerned.

(3) No Bill enacted by a Sultan with the advice of a Malay Advisory Council shall become a law until the Governor shall have assented thereto and shall have signed the same in token of assent.

(4) The provisions of paragraph (3) of section 31 and of section 32 of this Order (which relate respectively to the date of coming into operation of laws passed by the Legislative Council and to disallowance by His Majesty of such laws) shall apply to laws enacted by a Sultan with the advice of a Malay Advisory Council.

===== Page 518 =====
Qualifications for Membership.

78. Subject to the provisions of this Order, any person who is a Malayan Union citizen of the age of twenty-one years or upwards shall be qualified to be a Member of a Malay Advisory Council, and no other person shall be qualified to be appointed or, having been so appointed, shall sit or vote in such Council.

Disqualification for Membership.

79. A person shall be disqualified for membership of a Malay Advisory Council for any reason which would cause disqualification for Unofficial Membership of the Legislative Council under the provisions of section 21 of this Order.

Tenure.

80. (1) Subject to the provisions of this Order, a Member of a Malay Advisory Council shall hold his seat in the Council during the pleasure of the Sultan of the State and, subject thereto, shall vacate his seat at the expiration of three years from the date of his appointment or on dissolution of the Council whichever is earlier, but, if qualified, shall be eligible for re-appointment.

(2) If any person is appointed temporarily a Member in accordance with the provisions of section 82 of this Order and such appointment is immediately followed by his definitive appointment as a Member, the said period of three years shall be reckoned from the date of his appointment as a temporary member.

Vacation of seats.

81. (1) The seat of a Member of a Malay Advisory Council shall become vacant upon the occurrence in relation to such Member of any event which, if it occurred in relation to a Nominated Unofficial Member of the Legislative Council, would cause the seat of such Member to become vacant under the provisions of sub-section (2) of section 21 of this Order:

Provided that references to the Governor in that sub-section shall be deemed to be references to the Sultan of the State concerned.

===== Page 519 =====
(2) Whenever the seat of a Member becomes vacant under the provisions of sub-section (1) of this section, the vacancy shall forthwith be reported to the Resident Commissioner in writing by the Clerk of the Council concerned.

(3) The Sultan of the State concerned may, with the approval of the Governor, declare any Member to be incapable of discharging his functions as a Member of the Council, and thereupon such Member shall not sit or vote in the Council until he is declared by the Sultan, with like approval, again to be capable of discharging his said functions.

(4) Any person vacating a seat as a Member of a Malay Advisory Council may, if qualified, be again appointed from time to time.

82. (1) Whenever the seat of a Member of a Malay Advisory Council is temporarily vacant, the Sultan may appoint a qualified person to be temporarily a Member for the period of such vacancy.

(2) Every such temporary appointment shall be reported to the Resident Commissioner by the Clerk of the Council and may (without prejudice to anything done thereunder) be disallowed by the Governor.

(3) A temporary appointment under this section shall cease to have effect on notification by the Sultan or the Resident Commissioner to the person appointed of disallowance by the Governor of the appointment or of supersession of the appointment by the definitive appointment of a person to fill the vacancy, or when the vacancy shall otherwise cease to exist.

83. The Sultan of the State concerned, or in the absence of the Sultan, such Member of the Council as the Sultan may from time to time appoint or, in default of such appointment or, in the absence of the Member so [POSSIBLY MISSING].

===== Page 520 =====
84. The Members of each Malay Advisory Council shall take precedence among themselves as the Sultan of the State concerned may assign.

85. Whenever the seat of a Member becomes vacant, the vacancy shall be filled by appointment by the Sultan with the approval of the Governor.

86. All questions proposed for decision in the Council shall be determined by a majority of the votes of the numbers present and voting. The President shall not have an original vote, but if upon any question the votes shall be equally divided, he shall have a casting vote.

87. In the event of any conflict between a law enacted by a Sultan with the advice of a Malay Advisory Council and an Ordinance of the Legislative Council the provisions of the Ordinance shall prevail.

88. Subject to the provisions of this Order, each Sultan and each Malay Advisory Council shall, in the transaction of business and enacting of laws, conform as nearly as may be to the directions contained in any Instructions under His Majesty's Sign Manual and Signet which may, from time to time, be addressed to the Governor and to any Instructions of the Governor under the Public Seal which may from time to time be addressed to the Sultan or President of the Council in that behalf.

89. The provisions of section 34 of this Order (which relate to Standing Rules and Orders of the Legislative Council) shall apply also to Malay Advisory Councils.

90. The official language of each Malay Advisory Council shall be Malay.

91. (1) The Sessions of each Malay Advisory Council shall be held at such times and places as the Sultan shall from time to time appoint.

===== Page 521 =====
(2) There shall be a session of the Council at least once every year, so that a period of twelve months shall not intervene between the last sitting in one session and the first sitting in the next session.

92. (1) The Sultan of any Malay State, with the approval of the Governor, may at any time prorogue or dissolve the Malay Advisory Council in his State.

(2) On dissolution, all Members shall vacate their seats and the Council shall be reconstituted by appointment in accordance with the provisions of this Order at such time within six months of every dissolution as the Sultan, with the approval of the Governor, shall appoint.

Quorum: 1/4

A Malay Advisory Council shall not be disqualified for the transaction of business by region.

===== Page 522 =====
PART VIII TRANSITIONAL PROVISIONS

93. Subject to anything to the contrary contained in this Order, the Governor may, by Proclamation in the Gazette, declare that any provision of this Part shall cease to have effect, and thereupon such provision shall cease to have effect accordingly on the date specified in the Proclamation.

94. (1) Until such time as section 15 and Parts III, IV, V, VI and VII of this Order shall come into operation, it shall be lawful for the Governor to make laws for the peace, order and good government of the Union, and any such law may be made to operate retrospectively to any date prior to the appointed day.

(2) In making such laws the Governor shall consult the Advisory Council.

95. (1) The Advisory Council shall consist of the Chief Secretary, the Attorney General, the Financial Secretary and such other persons as the Governor may, by Instrument under the Public Seal, appoint; and such persons shall hold their places in the said Council during the Governor's pleasure.

(2) The Governor alone shall be entitled to submit questions to the Advisory Council for their advice. The Governor may act in opposition to the advice given to him by the Members of the Advisory Council if he shall in any case deem it right so to do. In such case, it shall be competent to any Member of the Advisory Council to require that there be recorded on the Minutes the grounds of any advice.

===== Page 523 =====
(3) The Governor may regulate the procedure and practice of the Advisory Council in such manner as he shall see fit.

96. (1) Subject to the provisions of this Order, the existing laws shall, except in so far as the same shall, in their application to the Union or to any part thereof, be repealed, amended or otherwise affected by or under any Act of Parliament, Order of His Majesty in Council or any law made by the Legislature of the Union or by the Governor or other lawful authority under the provisions of this Order, continue to have effect in the Union or in that part thereof to which such law applied prior to the appointed day, but shall be construed subject to such modifications as may be necessary to bring them into conformity with the provisions of the principal Act and of this Order.

(2) Without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing sub-section, the existing laws until repealed, amended or otherwise affected as aforesaid, shall, in their application to any part of the Union, be construed, when the context admits, subject to the following modifications:

(a) References to the Colony of the Straits Settlements shall be construed as referring (but not exclusively) to the Settlement of Malacca and the Settlement of Penang;

(b) References to the Federated Malay States shall be construed as references to the States of Negri Sembilan, Pahang, Perak and Selangor;

(c) References to the Governor, or to any official of the Colony of the Straits Settlements, or to the High Commissioner of the Malay States or to the Ruler of any Malay State or to

===== Page 524 =====
any authority or body formerly performing, or empowered or obliged to perform functions in the Straits Settlements or the Federated Malay States or any of the Malay States, shall be construed as references to the Governor, or to the corresponding official of the Union, or to the authority or body performing, or empowered or obliged to perform corresponding functions in the Union, as the case may be; and

(d) references to the Chief Justice or to any Court, Judge or Magistrate of the Straits Settlements or of the Federated Malay States or of any of the Malay States shall be construed as references to the Chief Justice or to the corresponding Court, Judge or Magistrate of the Union.

(3) The amendments specified in the second column of the Second Schedule to this Order shall be made in the Acts of Parliament and Orders in Council mentioned in the first column of that Schedule.

97. Subject to the provisions of this Order, nothing in this Order shall affect the validity or future operation of any lawful act done by any person or authority in the Union before the appointed day.

98. (1) All proceedings commenced before the 15th day of February 1942 in any Court of Justice in, or having jurisdiction in, the Union may be carried on in like manner as nearly as may be, as if this Order and the Act of 1946, had not been made or passed but in the corresponding Court of the Union, and any such proceeding may be amended in such manner as may appear necessary or proper in order to bring it into conformity with the provisions of the Act of 1946 and of this Order.

(2) All periods of limitation of actions, suits or other proceedings, whether they began to run before, on, or after the 15th day of February 1942, shall be treated

===== Page 525 =====
as having been suspended during a period commencing on the 15th day of February 1942 and ending on the day of 1943, or the appointed day.

99. The Supreme Court shall exercise full jurisdiction with respect to all crimes, offences and causes of action committed or arising before the appointed day within any of the Malay States or within the Settlement of Malacca or the Settlement of Penang or for which the offender might have been tried, or proceedings might have been had within any such State or Settlement, under the law in force in the said State in the Straits Settlements prior to the appointed day, and such jurisdiction shall be exercised in conformity with the law of the said State, or with the law of the Straits Settlements, as the case may be, in force in the State or Settlement concerned at the date when the crime or offence was committed or the cause of action arose and in accordance as nearly as may be, with the procedure in force in Courts constituted under this Order.

100. Military Courts established in the Malay Peninsula by the British Military Administration by virtue of any Proclamation made by, or under the authority of, the Supreme Allied Commander South East Asia Command, in which proceedings have been commenced and are pending at the date hereof shall be deemed to be special tribunals with jurisdiction to continue and conclude such proceedings, in accordance with such Proclamation after the appointed day and to issue valid warrants for the custody of prisoners and the carrying out of sentences. The powers and functions of a confirming authority or reviewing authority under any such Proclamation may be exercised by such person or persons as the Governor shall designate in that behalf.

i.e. the day on which the moratorium is lifted.

===== Page 526 =====
101 (1) Save as hereinafter provided, all property which, immediately before the appointed day, was vested in His Majesty, or in the Government of the Federated Malay States, or in the Ruler, or the Government of any of the Malay States, or in any officer of any such Government, for any of the purposes of any such Government, which will, after the appointed day, be a purpose of the Government of the Union, shall vest in His Majesty, or in the Government of the Union, or in the appropriate officer of that Government, as the case may be, for the purposes of the Government of the Union.

(2) Save as hereinafter provided, all property which, immediately before the appointed day, was vested in His Majesty in relation to His Government of the Straits Settlements or in such Government or in any officer of such Government solely for a purpose which will, after the appointed day, be a purpose of the Government of the Union in relation to the Settlement of Malacca or the Settlement of Penang or either of those Settlements, shall vest in His Majesty or in the Government of the Union or in the appropriate officer of that Government, as the case may be, for the purposes of the Government of the Union.

(3) Nothing in this section shall be taken to divest, transfer or vest property of any of the following descriptions which immediately prior to the appointed day was vested in His Majesty or in the Ruler of any of the Malay States, that is to say:

(a) any residence or estate held by, or in trust for, any Ruler for his personal use;

(b) property comprised in any Mohammedan religious endowment or waqf; or

(c) any property which may be declared to be excluded from the operation of this section by the Governor.

(4) The Governor may make regulations for determining in any case of doubt the person or body in whom property transferred by this section is vested, and generally for giving effect to this section, and may declare, in case of doubt whether any particular property is, or is not, excluded from the operation thereof.

===== Page 527 =====
(5) It shall be lawful for the Governor, with the approval of a Secretary of State, to make, and from time to time to vary, rescind or carry into effect arrangements with the Government of any territory which before the appointed day was included in the Straits Settlements but is not included in the Union, providing for the perfecting of any transfer of property effected by this section and for values to be assessed for such financial or other adjustment as may require to be made in consequence of any such transfer.

102. (1) All liabilities incurred by or on behalf of the Government of the Federated Malay States or of the Government of any of the Malay States for any purpose which will, after the appointed day, be a purpose of the Government of the Union shall be deemed to have been incurred by or on behalf of the Government of the Union.

(2) Without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, such liabilities shall include claims in respect of Loans issued, and Post Office Savings deposits received, by the Government of the Federated Malay States or the Government of any of the Malay States.

(3) Pensions, gratuities and other like allowances in respect of service under the Government of the Federated Malay States or the Government of any of the Malay States before the appointed day, whether awarded before or after that day, shall be liabilities deemed to have been incurred by or on behalf of the Government of the Union.

(4) All liabilities incurred by or on behalf of the Government of the Straits Settlements solely for a purpose which will, after the appointed day, be a purpose

===== Page 528 =====
A purpose of the Government of the Union in relation to the Settlement of Malacca and the Settlement of Penang or either of them shall be deemed to have been incurred by or on behalf of the Government of the Union.

(5) It shall be lawful for the Governor, with the approval of a Secretary of State, to make, and from time to time to vary, rescind or carry into effect, arrangements with the Governor of any territory which before the appointed day was included in the Straits Settlements but is not included in the Union, for the payment to the Government of that territory from the funds of the Government of the Union, of contributions towards the sums necessary to defray such of the liabilities of the Government of the Straits Settlements as shall have been transferred by any Order in Council to the Government of that territory including liabilities for the service and repayment of loans, and War Savings Certificates issued by, and Post Office Savings Deposits received by, the Government of the Straits Settlements. Such contributions shall be proportionate to the share of such liabilities which is properly attributable to the Settlement of Malacca and the Settlement of Penang.

(6) Claims in respect of liabilities deemed, by virtue of this section to have been incurred by or on behalf of the Government of the Union which, if the Act of 1946 had not been passed, and this Order had not been made, might have been enforced against His Majesty in His Government of the Straits Settlements, or against the Government of the Federated Malay States or of any of the Malay States or against any officer of any such Government in his official capacity or against any department of any such Government in connection with the administration of the Settlement of Malacca or of the Settlement of Penang or of the Federated Malay States.

===== Page 529 =====
or of any Malay State, may be enforced in such manner as may be prescribed by the law of the Union or by any regulation made by the Governor under sub-section (7) of this section, against His Majesty in His Government of the Union or against the Government or the appropriate officer of the Government of the Union, as the case may be.

(7) The Governor may make regulations for determining in any case of doubt the person or body by or against whom claims may be enforced under this section and generally for giving effect thereto.

The provisions of the Act may be amended if laws are made under Part IV of the Ordinance.

===== Page 530 =====
PART IX MISCELLANEOUS

Oath of Allegiance.

103. Except for the purpose of enabling this section to be complied with, no Member shall sit or vote on any Council constituted by this Order until he shall have taken and subscribed before the Governor, or some person authorised by the Governor in that behalf, the Oath of Allegiance in the form set out in the First Schedule to this Order:

Provided that every person authorised by law to make an affirmation instead of taking an oath in legal proceedings may make such affirmation in like terms instead of the said oath.

104. (1) Provision may be made by any law of the Union enacted under this Order and, until such provision has been made, by regulations made by the Governor regulating any matter left by this Order to be regulated by local law or by regulations made by the Governor.

(2) In particular and without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing sub-section, provision may be made regulating the election of Elected Members to any State Council or Settlement Council constituted by this Order including provision for regulating any of the following matters, that is to say:

(a) the persons, associations or bodies by whom elections are to be made;
(b) the holding of elections;
(c) the qualifications of voters;
(d) the registration of voters;
(e) the ascertainment of the qualifications of voters or candidates;
(f) offences in relation to elections including the trial and punishment thereof; and

===== Page 531 =====
(g) The disposal of election petitions and the determination of questions otherwise arising regarding membership of the Councils.

(3) Such regulations shall be published in the Gazette; and thereafter every such regulation shall take effect on the day of publication or on such other date as may be therein specified and shall have the force of law, subject to any revocation or amendment thereof by subsequent regulation or law.

105. Any person who

(a) having been elected or appointed a member of any Council constituted by this Order, but not having been at the time of such election or appointment, qualified to be so elected or appointed shall sit or vote in such Council; or

(b) shall sit or vote in such Council after his seat therein has become vacant or he has become disqualified from sitting or voting therein;

knowing, or having reasonable grounds for knowing, that he was so disqualified, or that his seat has become vacant, as the case may be, shall be liable to a penalty not exceeding twenty pounds for every day upon which he so sits or votes.

(2) The said penalty shall be recoverable by action in the Supreme Court at the suit of the Attorney General.

106. No Council constituted by this Order shall be disqualified from the transaction of business by reason of any vacancy among the Members, or by reason of the sitting or voting therein, of any unqualified or disqualified person.

===== Page 532 =====
107. The High Court shall have jurisdiction in all matters involving the interpretation of the Constitution.

108. (1) If any difficulty shall arise in bringing into operation any of the provisions of this Order or in giving effect to the purposes thereof, a Secretary of State may, by Order, make such provision as seems to him necessary or expedient for the purpose of removing the difficulty and may by such Order amend or add to any provision of this Order:

Provided that no Order under this section shall be made later than the first day of January, 1949.

(2) Any Order under this section may be amended, added to, or revoked by a further Order, and may be given retrospective effect to a day not earlier than the 15th day of February 1942.

(3) This section shall come into operation on the appointed day.

109. Nothing in this Order shall affect the power of His Majesty in Council to make laws from time to time for the peace, order and good government of the Colony.

NB Reservation from James Lamer as well as from the Order in the Act of 1946.

===== Page 533 =====
FIRST SCHEDULE
OATH OF ALLEGIANCE

(Sections 6 and 103)

I, ... do swear that I will be faithful and bear true allegiance to His Majesty King George VI, His Heirs and Successors, according to Law. So help me God.

OATH FOR THE DUE EXECUTION
OF THE OFFICE OF GOVERNOR.

I, ... do swear that I will well and truly serve His Majesty King George VI, His Heirs and Successors, in the Office of Governor. So help me God.

===== Page 534 =====
THE STRAITS SETTLEMENTS ORDER, 1946.

AT THE COURT AT
THE DAY OF 1946.

PRESENT

THE KING'S MOST EXCELLENT MAJESTY IN COUNCIL.

WHEREAS by the Straits Settlements (Repeal) Act, 1945 (hereinafter referred to as "the principal Act") it is, among other things, provided that on such day as His Majesty may by Order in Council appoint (in the principal Act and in this Order referred to as "the appointed day") the Straits Settlements Act, 1866 shall be thereby repealed and the Straits Settlements, that is to say the territories mentioned in the Schedule to the principal Act, shall cease to be a single colony and on and after the appointed day each of the said territories shall be governed as His Majesty may by Order in Council direct:

AND WHEREAS by the principal Act it is further provided that the British Settlements Acts 1887 and 1945 (which authorise His Majesty to make laws and establish institutions for British Settlements as defined in those Acts) shall apply in relation to each of the said territories as if it were a British Settlement as so defined, so however that no instrument under those Acts relating to any of the said territories shall come into operation before the appointed day:

AND WHEREAS it is expedient to bring into operation the principal Act, to the intent that laws may be made and institutions established for the said territories:

NOW THEREFORE His Majesty, in exercise of the powers conferred on Him by the principal Act and of all other powers enabling Him in that behalf, is pleased, by and with the advice of His Privy Council, to order, and it is hereby ordered, as follows:-

===== Page 535 =====
1. This Order may be cited as the Straits Settlements Order in Council, 1946.

2. The day of 1946 shall be the appointed day and as from that day the principal Act shall come into operation.
